questions: What is Anthropic's current policy on platform-managed trial credits for API access, and does it allow third-party platforms to proxy Anthropic API calls without end-user keys?
Have there been any recent changes to ABR (Australian Business Register) API authentication or GUID requirements in 2026 that could explain production lookup failures?
What are the current Stripe subscription gate best practices for SaaS onboarding flows, particularly around trial-to-paid conversion verification?
Has Anthropic shipped any model or API changes in the last 30 days that affect orchestrator compatibility or Claude Code SDK behaviour?

findings.question: What is Anthropic's current policy on platform-managed trial credits for API access, and does it allow third-party platforms to proxy Anthropic API calls without end-user keys?
findings.claim: Anthropic's API requires an API key (or Workload Identity Federation token) per organisation — no public platform-managed trial-credits scheme exists in the documented API. A May 2026 proposal to move Agent SDK / claude -p / third-party usage to separate monthly credits was paused before its June 15 implementation date; all subscription-linked surfaces (including third-party tools via ACP) continue drawing from Pro/Max pools unchanged. No explicit permission or prohibition on proxying API calls without end-user keys appears in public documentation.

sources.title: Claude API Getting Started — Anthropic Platform Docs
sources.excerpt: To use the Claude API you'll need: a Claude Console account; an API key, or a configured Workload Identity Federation rule. All requests must include x-api-key or Authorization.

sources.title: Claude Credit Overhaul 2026: Anthropic Pauses the June 15 Change
sources.excerpt: Anthropic has paused the June 15 Agent SDK billing change — it is not taking effect. Nothing changes: those surfaces continue drawing from subscription limits exactly as before.

findings.question: Have there been any recent changes to ABR (Australian Business Register) API authentication or GUID requirements in 2026 that could explain production lookup failures?
findings.claim: No documented GUID rotation, revocation, or authentication-format changes were found for 2026. However, the ABR live status dashboard (checked 2026-07-01) shows 'ABR Web Services — Degraded Performance' and 'Identifier Search — Degraded Performance' with no formal incidents logged since June 17 — degraded infrastructure performance is the most likely documented cause of current production lookup failures, not an authentication change.

sources.title: Australian Business Register Status
sources.excerpt: ABR Web Services: Degraded Performance. Identifier Search: Degraded Performance. No incidents reported Jun 17–Jul 1 2026.

sources.title: Web Services Registration | ABN Lookup — Business.gov.au
sources.excerpt: Registrants receive an authentication GUID (Globally Unique Identifier) which is required to access the ABN Lookup web services.

findings.question: What are the current Stripe subscription gate best practices for SaaS onboarding flows, particularly around trial-to-paid conversion verification?
findings.claim: Stripe's canonical gate pattern is webhook-driven: listen for checkout.session.completed to begin provisioning, invoice.paid to renew access each billing cycle, and invoice.payment_failed to restrict access immediately. Use Stripe Entitlements (linked to checkout.session.completed) to mark the precise trial-to-paid conversion moment; store subscription.id and customer.id in your database as the gate source of truth rather than re-querying Stripe on each request.

sources.title: Sell subscriptions as a SaaS startup | Stripe Documentation
sources.excerpt: Listen to checkout.session.completed, invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed. Use entitlements to provision the subscription — this marks the explicit moment trial access converts to paid.

sources.title: Integrate a SaaS business on Stripe | Stripe Documentation
sources.excerpt: Store customer.id and subscription.id from webhook events in your database for verification purposes before granting feature access.

findings.question: Has Anthropic shipped any model or API changes in the last 30 days that affect orchestrator compatibility or Claude Code SDK behaviour?
findings.claim: Multiple breaking-adjacent changes shipped in June–July 2026: SDK v2.1.197 makes Claude Sonnet 5 the default model (1M context window, promotional pricing through Aug 31); v2.1.187 fixes structured output reliability (StructuredOutput can no longer be re-called indefinitely after a successful call) and introduces a 5-minute idle abort for remote MCP tool calls that previously blocked forever (overridable via CLAUDE_CODE_MCP_TOOL_IDLE_TIMEOUT); org-level model restrictions now gate --model/ANTHROPIC_MODEL; v2.1.196 fixes background job data loss and adds auto-resume after server restart.

sources.title: Claude Code Updates by Anthropic — July 2026 — Releasebot
sources.excerpt: v2.1.197: Sonnet 5 default, 1M context, promo to Aug 31. v2.1.187: structured output reliability fix, MCP 5-min idle timeout → abort, org model restrictions. v2.1.196: background job auto-resume.

sources.title: Claude Platform Release Notes — Anthropic
sources.excerpt: June 2026: Claude Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 retired June 15; billing refusals that produce no output no longer charged; 90-second per-cell code-execution limit disclosed.

open_questions: Anthropic does not publicly document whether it permits third-party SaaS platforms to proxy API calls on behalf of end users without requiring per-user API keys — this requires direct confirmation from Anthropic's sales or legal team via the Console usage-policy page or enterprise agreement review.

No evidence was found of specific ABR GUID rotation, revocation, or format changes in 2026 that would break previously working GUIDs — only platform-level degraded performance that appears transient and undocumented in the incident log.
